title: Optional PII-free family feedback mechanism
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. A backlog/future design-spec for an OPTIONAL, PII-free family feedback mechanism; it requires a fresh privacy review before any data path is built, and must collect no PII. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Specify an optional, strictly PII-free family feedback mechanism, gated on a fresh privacy review before any data path is implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: Specifies an optional feedback mechanism that collects no PII and no engagement/tracking metrics
Requires a fresh privacy review before any data path is built; documents the data-minimization design and retention/deletion approach
Aligns with the no-tracking, static-delivery stance; surfaces qualitative usefulness only
